title: Set registry row — one TSV row in set_registry.tsv
description: Flat catalogue row, one per registered set. set_registry.tsv has these columns in order (tab-separated, header on the first line). Use TSV for grep / pandas / shell-piping; use the per-set JSON (set_v1.schema.json) under sets/<entity_type>/<set_id>.json for the full structured form (derived_from, set algebra, predicate, etc.). Per SETS_AND_ANALYSES.md §'The TSV catalogues'.

entity_type.description: Kind of thing in the set. Per entity_type_v1.

path.description: Relative or absolute path to the members file. Empty when the set is derived (definition_path points at the JSON).

n_entities.description: Member count. Empty allowed (recomputed by lib/registry_index.py when missing).

hash.description: sha256:<hex> of canonical members. Empty allowed for derived sets that haven't been materialized.

parent_set_id.description: Single-parent FK, empty if no parent or if derived_from with multiple parents (use definition_path then).

filter_profile_id.description: Optional filter profile name. Empty allowed.

coordinate_system.description: Genome assembly id when entity_type is coordinate_aware. Empty for sample / bam / chromosome.

definition_path.description: Path to the per-set JSON (set_v1) when the set has set-algebra lineage (derived_from with multiple parents, filter predicate, etc.). Empty for atomic sets where everything fits in TSV columns.

_tsv_empty_value: Empty cells are rendered as the string '' (zero-length, NOT 'NA'/'.'/'NULL') so split('\t') has the right column count. The parser fills missing fields with None.
